newsSinceJun6: Schall Law Firm announced (Jun 8) an investigation into potential fiduciary-duty breaches by directors/management, layered on top of the existing financial-restatement overhang (non-reliance 8-K from Feb 26, reiterated Apr 30). Disclosures show ~$380M of insider sales in late May (CEO Hsing ~$240M, EVPs Tseng ~$96M and Sciammas ~$47M). Stock whipsawed: +5.3% Jun 8, -4.63% Jun 9 as the rally was faded on the governance overhang. Annual meeting Jun 11 includes EY auditor ratification vote. No new earnings, design wins, or capacity news; the AI power-delivery thesis itself is unchanged.

sources.label: Quiver Quantitative - MPWR slips on restatement overhang and insider-selling disclosures
PRNewswire - Schall Law Firm investigation of Monolithic Power Systems
TradingKey - MPWR moved up 6.38% on Jun 8, facts behind the movement
StockTitan - MPWR 2026 definitive proxy (annual meeting Jun 11, EY ratification)

verdicts.rankRationale: On a residual-alpha list, a fresh fiduciary-duty investigation plus ~$380M of insider selling at peak valuation is new negative evidence layered on an already 'crowded/rerated benchmark' name; today's -4.6% is governance-driven, not a thesis-intact flush that improves asymmetry.
verdicts.bull: VRM/vertical power content per accelerator keeps rising and MPWR remains hard to substitute at high current densities; any cleared restatement removes the overhang quickly.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but fully recognized at ~$76B cap and +59% YTD; the governance noise mostly adds volatility without changing the AI power TAM.
verdicts.bear: Restatement scope could widen, the investigation invites litigation and auditor friction (EY vote Jun 11), and insiders dumping $380M signals they see full value.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Restatement resolution / clean re-filed financials, plus next-gen accelerator power-stage design wins.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Restatement scope widening or auditor/governance escalation forcing institutional de-risking.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already cut it to 56 on directness grounds (less direct than HBM/packaging/networking); the Schall investigation and insider-sale disclosures are governance noise that does not change its node-bottleneck directness, so no second consecutive move.
verdicts.bull: Power delivery scales with every node and accelerator generation, keeping MPWR structurally levered to AI compute growth regardless of governance headlines.
verdicts.neutral: Analog/power exposure is real but second-order versus HBM/CPO/test bottlenecks; rank 56 already reflects that.
verdicts.bear: Governance overhang compounds an elevated multiple, and analog/power cyclicality offers less downside protection than true chokepoint names.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Content gains in 800V/vertical power delivery for next-gen AI racks.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Restatement fallout plus analog-cycle softness hitting an elevated multiple.

verdicts.rankRationale: Already demoted to rank 90 yesterday with CPO-specific exposure flagged low and valuation flagged high; the new governance headlines are consistent with that placement and there is little room or reason to move again.
verdicts.bull: If CPO drives rack power density even higher, MPWR's high-current power management benefits indirectly across the board.
verdicts.neutral: CPO linkage stays tenuous; this is a generic AI power-density beneficiary near the bottom of a CPO-specific list, correctly placed.
verdicts.bear: High valuation plus restatement/investigation overhang with no CPO-specific catalyst leaves nothing pulling the rank up.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any disclosed power-stage content in CPO/optical-engine reference designs.
verdicts.primaryRisk: De-rating from governance escalation with no CPO catalyst to offset it.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday already moved it down for recognized rerating; since then the +5.3%/-4.6% whipsaw nets roughly flat and the Schall investigation is incremental governance noise, not a thesis change, so the rank holds.
verdicts.bull: Board-level power-delivery content per AI server keeps compounding and MPWR's execution record remains strong despite the accounting noise.
verdicts.neutral: AI PDN exposure is genuine but the market already pays for it at ~$76B; governance overhang and rerating offset each other near current rank.
verdicts.bear: Insider selling of ~$380M, a restatement still unresolved since February, and a fiduciary-duty probe are exactly the cocktail that precedes multi-quarter multiple compression.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Clean restatement resolution and Q2 guidance confirming AI power content growth.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Restatement scope widening or SEC involvement turning governance noise into a fundamental problem.
